name: Cal Poly Student Chapter of the Association for Women in Mathematics
summary: A society that aims to support women and minorities in mathematics and to influence the culture of mathematics as a whole.

name: Cal Poly Accounting Club
summary: CPAC helps students develop professionally and connect with job opportunities in the accounting profession. CPAC creates a community of students and professionals who give back and help students progress into their careers.

name: Human Resource Management Association Club
summary: The Human Resources & Management Association is open to all majors with a focus on professional development. We want to offer the students at Cal Poly a place to grow and learn about business, Human Resources, and management.

name: Student Association for Fire Ecology
summary: SAFE is focused on fostering a community of individuals from any major who are interested in fire ecology. We provide networking opportunities to acquire and prepare for summer positions and post-graduate careers in fire.

name: Women Involved in Software and Hardware
summary: WISH is a community of women, non-binary folks, & allies in computing majors (EE/CPE/CSC/SE/LAES) that promote the recruitment and retention of women in tech. Our club provides mentorship, technical, networking, and social opportunities for our members!

name: Cal Poly Sustainable Fashion Club
summary: This club will be used to inform students of the benefits of sustainable fashion practices, celebrate creativity, and create space for students of all intersecting identities. Our three main goals are education, artistry, and community.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma, Gamma Zeta Chapter
summary: Alpha Chi Sigma is a co-ed, professional chemistry fraternity committed to creating a positive environment where students and faculty interested in the chemical sciences can become involved in service and leadership.

name: Alpha Phi Omega, Zeta Omicron Chapter
summary: We are a co-ed national service fraternity, so our main goal is to do community service. Other than service, we also have fellowship events, interchapter events, alumni events, and more.

name: Alpha Rho Chi
summary: Professional fraternity aiming to organize and unite students of the CAED for education and professional development purposes to promote the artistic, scientific, and practical proficiency of its membership and the profession.

name: Alpha Pi Mu at Cal Poly
summary: We are the national honor society for Industrial Engineering students. We recognize the leaders in our department and give back to our community on and off campus.

name: Epsilon Omega Chapter of Alpha Chi Omega
summary: Alpha Chi Omega was founded to provide a basis of mutual helpfulness and of congenial fellowship. The purpose of Alpha Chi Omega was to provide the advancement of the intellectual, social and moral culture of its members.

name: Alpha Sigma Phi Iota Chi (ΑΣΦ IX)
summary: The purpose of Alpha Sigma Phi is to Better the Man through the creation and perpetuation of brotherhood founded upon the values of character: Silence, Charity, Purity, Honor, Patriotism.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi Professional Business Fraternity
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is a Co-Ed Professional Business Fraternity opened to all majors.

name: Epsilon Chi Chapter of Alpha Phi International Fraternity Incorporated
summary: Alpha Phi is a sisterhood of outstanding women supporting one another in lifelong achievement. The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 164 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members.

name: Air Conditioning and Refrigeration Club
summary: The Cal Poly Chapter of ASHRAE (American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers) helps students better understand the possibilities within the HVAC industry.

name: Alpha Sigma Mu
summary: Honors organization for the Materials Engineering Department.

name: Cal Poly Astronomical Society
summary: The Cal Poly Astro Society welcomes all who have a passion for space, no prior experience necessary. Throughout the year, we organize stargazing events, guest speakers, and quarterly camping trips. Hope to see you all on our next astronomical adventures!

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
summary: We host speakers from the industry once or twice a month to share about different opportunities within the field or lecture on interesting subjects within the aerospace industry.

name: CAED Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Club
summary: The CAED DEI Club seeks to improve and advocate for the academic, social, and professional circumstances of underrepresented and minority students within CAED as a way of increasing representation and active participation in college affairs.

name: Graduate Planning Students Association
summary: The Purpose of the Graduate Planning Students Association is to explore, promote, and support academic understanding, professional development, and the overall advancement of the field of city and regional planning.

name: Cal Geo Club
summary: The goal of Cal Geo is to foster professional relationships between students and geotechnical fields, while also driving interest in geology and environmental conservation. Cal Geo will maintain a professional environment while engaging in the outdoors.

name: Power and Energy Society
summary: Power and Energy Society works to build Cal Poly students' knowledge of the Electric Power industry through opportunities to interact with industrial professionals, industry tours, information sessions, and technical talks.

name: MBA Student Association
summary: The purpose of this organization is to create opportunities for Cal Poly MBA students to further their educational and professional development and be a resource for students to connect to professionals within the business world.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: SWE is a collegiate section of the international organization that works to promote gender equality in engineering through professional development, community outreach, and on-campus membership activities.

name: Undergraduate Law Association
summary: Cal Poly Undergraduate Law Association provides resources and information about law school and careers in the legal field.

name: Black Student Union
summary: A club for black students and their allies to engage in fun and educational social activities.

name: Cal Poly Agribusiness Management/National Agri-Marketing Association
summary: The ABM/NAMA club is a great way for students to learn about and/or prepare for a future in the agribusiness industry. The club allows students to attend industry conventions, compete in marketing competitions, and network with agribusiness professionals

name: American Society of Landscape Architecture, Student Chapter
summary: This organization helps students pursuing landscape architecture to learn more about the field, build connections, and engage in educational and professional events.

name: College of Science and Mathematics Ambassadors
summary: The Cal Poly College of Science and Mathematics Ambassadors is a group of dedicated students who are eager to be involved in COSAM by giving tours to prospective students, and organize/participate in diversity and outreach events.

name: American Marketing Association
summary: The American Marketing Association aims to provide students interested in the fields of marketing and sales with the tools and tips needed to create a successful career and future.

name: Society of Manufacturing Engineers
summary: Our mission is to connect students of all majors at Cal Poly through manufacturing and to provide a place on unity on campus.

name: Theta Alpha chapter of Delta Gamma Fraternity
summary: Our primary purpose is to foster high ideals of friendship, promote educaional & cultural interests, and develop the finest qualities of character

name: Sprout Up
summary: Sprout Up provides free, youth-led environmental education programs to 1st & 2nd grade classrooms in public schools throughout California and New York City.

name: Delta Sigma Pi Kappa Mu Chapter
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities and promote closer affiliation between the commercial world and students of commerce.

name: The Gordon Student Chapter of the American Institute of Floral Designers, Inc.
summary: Club members learn the fundamentals of floral design and; have the opportunity to compete in State and; National AIFD student competitions. Students also attend industry leader workshops and create arrangements for various Cal Poly events

name: Associated General Contractors
summary: We are the Cal Poly student chapter of the Associated General Contractors organization. We connect students interested in general contracting and construction with the industry, we offer professional development, networking, and volunteer opportunities.

name: Design Lab
summary: Design Lab provides a space for designers and professionals to connect, collaborate on projects, and learn more about each other and the industry.

name: Her Campus at Cal Poly
summary: We are an online magazine catered to collegiate women but open to all. Joining our magazine is a great way to get real experience in the editorial, media, journalism, marketing, and event planning industry.

name: Associated Students in Planning
summary: Cal Poly ASP is a professional club for CRP (City and Regional Planning) majors and non-majors to enhance their academic careers by helping to expose them to the City Planning profession. Meetings are weekly. Please contact calpolyasp@gmail.com for more.

name: College of Liberal Arts Ambassadors
summary: CLA Ambassadors represent the College of Liberal Arts to prospective, current, and past students.

name: Cal Poly Real Estate Club
summary: Club exposes students at Cal Poly to the many different facets of within the Real Estate industry.

name: Information Systems Association
summary: Information Systems Association(ISA) is a professional networking club under OCOB that helps connect companies and students for jobs and internship opportunities.

name: The Cal Poly Pre-Law Chapter
summary: Phi Alpha Delta is a professional, co-ed, pre-law fraternity for aspiring law students who want to gain more knowledge about professionalism, brotherhood, and philanthropy as it relates to the law and to lawyers.

name: Hack4Impact
summary: We are a student-run 501(c)(3) organization that empowers engineers, designers, activists, and humanitarians to create lasting social change by developing projects for local nonprofits. We foster the wider adoption of software as a tool for social good.

name: Structural Engineers Association of California Cal Poly Student Chapter
summary: SEAOC serves as a professional club for students interested in the Architectural Engineering/Structural Engineering Field.

name: Thai Vietnamese Student Association
summary: The Thai-Vietnamese Student Association is a socio-cultural club that aims to promote Thai and Vietnamese culture and build a strong community through various events and activities.

name: Mustang Aviation Club
summary: The Mustang Aviation Club advances the science of aeronautics, to develop aviation and to educate pilots in every way towards safe operations.

name: Associated Students of Construction Management
summary: ASCM offers students the opportunity to learn and be involved in the construction management profession, to network and develop relationships with potential employers, and meet fellow students.

name: Cal Poly Entrepreneurs
summary: CPE is a thriving student entrepreneurial community on campus that brings together creative thinkers and doers to build world-changing ideas. Join us Tuesdays 6-7 pm in the Nash Innovation Lab.

name: Cal Poly Cat Program Club
summary: The Cal Poly Cat Program's mission is to provide a safe and nurturing environment for sanctuary, surrendered, stray, and rehabilitated feral cats and kittens whilst they wait for new loving homes.

name: Cal Poly Women's Club Water Polo
summary: The Cal Poly Club Women's Water polo team is a group of athletes that compete at tournaments in the Pacific Coast Division of the Collegiate Water Polo Association. We are an inclusive team that collectively shares the love for the game.

name: Association of Environmental Professionals
summary: The Cal Poly Student Chapter of Association of Environmental Professionals promotes networking and professional development in the field of natural resources management in order to enhance, maintain, and protect the natural and human environment.

name: Environmental Sciences Club
summary: ESC serves to introduce students who are interested in Environmental Science to potential career paths, community service organizations, and recreational activities related to environmental science and natural resources.

name: Chi Epsilon Honor Society
summary: Chi Epsilon is the National Civil Engineering Honor Society in the United States. We honor students who have exemplified the principles of "Scholarship, Character, Practicality, and Sociability" in the civil and environmental engineering profession.

name: Association of Transfer Students
summary: The Association of Transfer Students (ATS) is a social club designed to help transfers build community and make their transition to our campus as fun and easy as possible.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
summary: IISE is dedicated to the professional development, education, and involvement of all our members. Some of our events include info sessions with top industry representatives, socials, career fairs, and workshops. We welcome everyone whos interested!

name: Bailey College of Science and Mathematics Advisory Council
summary: The Bailey College of Science and Mathematics Advisory Council provides a line of communication between students, student organizations in the college, and the faculty and staff.

name: InterFraternity Council
summary: The governing body of Cal Poly Social Fraternities.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
summary: Electrical and Computer Engineering Honor Society

name: Cal Poly Security Education Club
summary: We are a group of security-oriented individuals who aim to teach and develop our security expertise through weekly talks, hands-on activities and competitions, and interdisciplinary research. Open to all majors!

name: Packaging Club
summary: The Packaging Club (PKG) club aims to connect students in the packaging concentration with peers, professors, alumni, and professionals and offer insight on career development within the packaging industry.

name: 𝚪𝚭𝚨 Gamma Zeta Alpha Fraternity Inc. - Omicron Chapter
summary: The Omicron Chapter of Gamma Zeta Alpha Fraternity, Inc. is a Latino interest fraternity that fosters an environment where brothers can succeed academically and professionally while uplifting underrepresented communities.

name: Cal Poly Investing Club
summary: Cal Poly Investing was founded to give Cal Poly students of all years and majors a place to learn about and discuss investing of all kinds.

name: SLO Smash Community
summary: Club dedicated to the discussion, celebration, and tournaments for the Super Smash Bros game series and other, similar games.

name: Cal Poly Mycology Club
summary: A club dedicated to allowing students to learn about the field of mycology. Major activities include foraging trips, mushroom grows, and other research projects. Join our Discord for information: https://discord.gg/yc9AMaWfaf

name: PolyCon Tabletop Gaming
summary: PolyCon Tabletop Gaming hosts weekly gaming events where members play board games, card games, and tabletop RPGs. We also run one gaming convention each quarter, with fall and winter being one day events and spring being a whole weekend.

name: Can of Worms Comedy Club
summary: Can of Worms Comedy Club is the club for you if you want to venture out into the dazzling world of stand-up comedy! Whether you like to watch or perform stand-up comedy Can of Worms has open mics, themed nights, workshops, and showcases all year round!

name: Association for Materials Protection and Performance - Cal Poly Chapter
summary: The AMPP California Central Coast Student Chapter is a group of hardworking students looking outside the classroom to expand their education and network within the corrosion, coatings, and electrochemistry industries.

name: Net Impact
summary: Net Impact is a community dedicated to empowering students with opportunities to learn about and engage with the power of sustainable business as an agent for change.

name: Design Village
summary: Design Village is a student-run organization that hosts annual design-build competitions under the direct sponsorship of Cal Poly's CAED. Competitors across the country compete alongside Cal Poly students to claim winner of the year's theme.

name: National Electrical Contractors Association
summary: Cal Poly National Electrical Contractors Association, student chapter.

name: Communication Studies Club
summary: Cal Poly COMS Club is a dynamic organization designed for students who are interested in understanding the communication field with a focus on academic, professional, and social experiences.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: NSBE's mission is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: Military Science Club (MSC)
summary: MSC is a social group where CP students and ROTC cadets can come together for friendly gatherings, compete in entertaining games, participate in volunteer events, and have many opportunities to relax and have some fun!

name: Comparative Ethnic Studies Student Association
summary: Comparative Ethnic Studies Student Association's purpose is to provide a place to supplement the educational and networking experiences of students with an interest in Comparative Ethnic Studies.

name: Cal Poly Juggling Club
summary: We meet to learn new tricks and props, practice our old ones, and spread the joy of juggling by teaching others.

name: Club 34
summary: Club 34 is Cal Poly's social art club. We aim to build a community of interdisciplinary creatives by hosting a handful of social events each quarter which may include student-run shows, craft nights, artist talks, and movie nights.

name: Color Coded
summary: Founded in 2018, "Color Coded: Diversity in Tech" is a student-led organization aiming to promote the professional and academic success of underrepresented students in the technology industry.

name: The Nightcap
summary: The Nightcap is Cal Poly's only sketch comedy club! We create videos and live performances over the course of the term, and then showcase them in one big show that is open to the entire campus.

name: Women in Law
summary: Women in Law provides women and gender-diverse students opportunities to learn from legal professionals, build community, and access resources on law school admissions, internships, and career development, preparing members for success in the legal field

name: Studio Art Club
summary: We are an all inclusive club that houses a variety of majors. We welcome anyone in the Art & Design department and anyone else interested in expanding their knowledge or getting more involved in the arts on & off campus.

name: Cal Poly Surf Team
summary: The Cal Poly Surf Team competes in the NSSA Southwest College Division and is open to advanced surfers who qualify through tryouts. We’re committed to competitive excellence, integrity, and team unity, while fostering a culture of equality and respect.

name: Mat Pica Pi
summary: Mat Pica Pi is Graphic Communication's social and professional development club. We aim to connect all generations of GrC, as well as connect our students with professional career opportunities.

name: Cal Poly Surfrider Foundation
summary: We are a student chapter of Surfrider Foundation, a national non-profit organization dedicated to the protection and enjoyment of the world's oceans, beaches, and waves for all. We have many programs that focus on education and advocacy for our coastline

name: Mechanical Contractors Association of America
summary: MCAA competes in several mechanical competitions, fundraises with industry, and travels to conferences. Our Team simulates proposals of a mechanical and plumbing contractor and provides a unique and informative understanding of specialty contracting.

name: Sales Engineering Club
summary: Cal Poly SEC serves to increase student awareness and interest of sales engineering as a rewarding career path.

name: MESS
summary: The Materials Engineering Student Society hosts events for MATE students and offers opportunities to collaborate, study, socialize, and gain information about career options and research.

name: Math Club
summary: Math Club serves as an organized and scheduled social hour where all math-associated students of Cal Poly meet to fraternize and make connections.

name: Phi Alpha Theta at Cal Poly
summary: Phi Alpha Theta is a professional society for students interested in history. We promote the interest in history and connect students and faculty together through academic and social events.

name: College of Engineering Ambassadors
summary: The CENG Ambassadors are an enthusiastic and knowledgeable team of students who actively represent the College of Engineering through campus tours and public relations roles at request.

name: Systems Optimization Club
summary: The Systems Optimization Club strives to develop students professionally and socially through engineering-consulting projects, industry-relevant workshops, and networking opportunities.

name: Semper Fidelis Society
summary: The Semper Fi Society connects students inspired by Marine Corps values through mentorship, leadership development, and service. We build lasting bonds, strengthen character, and support those pursuing or honoring the Marine Corps path.

name: Society for the Advancement of Material & Process Engineering
summary: Research and Development with the use of Composite Materials. Common application and advisement for use of Carbon Fiber, Fiberglass, and green composite materials for other clubs.

name: Engineering Student Council
summary: The Engineering Student Council bridges communication between engineering students, the College of Engineering, and the University. ESC also fosters collaboration among diverse student organizations within the College of Engineering.

name: Pi Alpha Xi
summary: To promote the learning and love of horticulture and crop science through plants.

name: Pilipino Cultural Exchange
summary: The Pilipino Cultural Exchange (PCE) prides itself on providing a "home away from home" to anyone who seeks it. You do NOT need to be Filipino to join! All are welcome.

name: The Honor Society of Sigma Lambda Alpha
summary: Cal Poly San Luis Obispo chapter of Sigma Lambda Alpha, an academic honor society for landscape architecture students excelling in their academic success after completion of 50% of coursework.

name: Tau Beta Pi
summary: Tau Beta Pi is the National Engineering Honor Society. Tau Beta Pi seeks to recognize and serve those that have excelled in their area of study as well as demonstrated exemplary character in their education and profession.

name: Sigma Nu Kappa Pi Chapter
summary: A fraternity dedicated to professionalism, and philanthropic activities both on and off campus. We live by our motto love, truth & honor and make sure all of our members uphold these values in their everyday lives.

name: Society of American Foresters
summary: We are the Cal Poly student chapter of the Society of American Foresters, and we work to build relationships with industry professionals and our fellow students.

name: Society of Environmental Engineers
summary: Professional organization that introduces students to industry professionals and possibilities after graduation.

name: SHPE
summary: The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) recruits, retains and graduates the next generation of Hispanic engineers.

name: Cal Poly TAGA Club
summary: Cal Poly TAGA, or Technical Association of the Graphic Arts, is a hands-on, competitive Graphic Communication club that focuses on designing and producing a technical research journal and introducing students to the graphics for printing industry.

name: Cal Poly Psi Chi Chapter
summary: Psi Chi is the National Honor Society for psychology students. Psi Chi is a platform for students to form meaningful connections with peers and faculty members, explore career paths, and network within our community and otherwise.

name: Cal Poly Linux Users Group
summary: We're both users and fans of the GNU/Linux Operating System, and we want you to be too. We have regular talks and meetings, as well as social events when the opportunity arises.

name: Marine Sciences Club
summary: The Marine Sciences Club works to unite students across all disciplines who have a passion for marine sciences. We aim to build community, offer members connections to opportunities, and have fun!

name: New Student & Transition Programs
summary: Cal Poly Orientation is a tradition that has helped welcome new Mustangs to campus, connect students, parents and supporters and community members to Cal Poly, and build life-long relationships for over 60 years.

name: Campus Health & Wellbeing
summary: Campus Health  Wellbeing

name: Parent and Family Programs
summary: Parent and Family Programs provides support, resources and exciting programming for the proud parents, family members and supporters of Cal Poly students.

name: RISE
summary: We are a club that supports undocumented students at Cal Poly.

name: Students' Stage
summary: We’re Cal Poly’s only theatre club on campus! Students’ Stage has been a platform for students and theatre-lovers to collaborate and have fun doing theatre. Our doors are open to theatre practitioners of all backgrounds and every level.

name: Cal Poly Ducks Unlimited Club
summary: This club will supply a gateway for students to become involved with wetland management and waterfowl conservation. The club will also volunteer for Ducks Unlimited to help put on dinners and raffles to support wetland conservation nationwide

name: Cal Poly Scholars
summary: The Cal Poly Scholars program seeks to support and retain high-achieving students from California schools, who come from low-income backgrounds, by providing financial, academic, and community resources.

name: Disability Resource Center
summary: The Disability Resource Center (DRC) cultivates an accessible and inclusive community where students with permanent and temporary disabilities have an equal opportunity to participate in all aspects of campus life.

name: Safer
summary: Safer is Cal Poly’s primary confidential resource for addressing sexual assault, intimate partner violence, domestic violence, stalking, sexual exploitation and harassment.

name: Week of Welcome
summary: Hosted by New Student & Transition Programs

name: Cal Poly's Textile Engineering club
summary: The goal of Cal Poly's Textile Engineering club is to represent synthetic materials in engineering and provide opportunities to develop our own textiles. Students aim to establish connections with industries that work with polymers, fibers, and plastics.

name: Future Math Educators
summary: The primary goal of the club is increasing communication, fostering social connections, and the creation and upkeep of a community among students, faculty, and staff at Cal Poly interested in the study of the teaching of mathematics.

name: Cal Poly Computer Engineering Society
summary: We are a social and educational community for the Computer Engineering (as well as all computing) majors of Cal Poly! We are a student-run society whose primary goal is to help students succeed in their major and get a jump-start into the workforce.

name: Cal Poly Multiracial Students' Association
summary: MUSA is a space for mixed-race students and allies to explore identity, share experiences, and connect. Join us for beach trips, craft nights, and real talk. All are welcome, come find your place and your people!

name: Cal Poly Classical Literature Club
summary: The Cal Poly Classical Literature Club is a discussion-based club aiming to help members reach a better understanding of modern art, language, and media through reading classics.

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (oSTEM)
summary: oSTEM provides a space for LGBTQ+ persons pursuing STEM degrees to connect and thrive through outreach events, professional development, and community building among individuals who are passionate about the intersection of the queer experience and STEM.

name: Model Aeronautics
summary: New Club

name: CORE
summary: Core is a pre-WOW program rooted in proactively connecting underrepresented students to the resources, community, and connections that will kickstart their leadership journey at Cal Poly instilling confidence in their ability to navigate their WoW.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
summary: The AIAS is the country's largest organization for students of architecture, providing the best programs, information and resources available. The AIAS is the student affiliate of the AIA and your link to professionals in architecture.

name: Cal Poly Fighting Game Club
summary: A general fighting game community encompassing traditional fighters, platform fighters, etc.

name: Cal Poly Red Cross Club
summary: The Cal Poly Red Cross Club strives to extend the humanitarian efforts of the American Red Cross to the Cal Poly and SLO communities. Our certification classes, blood drives, and visits from healthcare professionals promote a safer and healthier campus.

name: Cal Poly Van Life Club
summary: A Van Life/ Tiny-House based club with a focus on travel, recreation & alternative living. Members also discuss DIY camper conversions. Ownership of a vehicle is not required to participate and all are welcome!

name: iter8 at Cal Poly
summary: We are Cal Poly's first product design agency for students to gain hands-on Product Design experience. We create a positive space for students to collaborate in teams, design user experience solutions for startups, and develop their portfolios.

name: Gender-Inclusive Gaming
summary: Gender-Inclusive Gaming is a social safe space that supports minorities who are interested in gaming of any variation as a leisure activity!

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon California Tau
summary: SigEp is redefining fraternity on college campuses across the country. We complement a man’s education by delivering a premier student experience in one of the most formative times of his life. Through SigEp, men strengthen their character, build leaders

name: Society for Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics & Native Americans in Science, California Polytechnic State University
summary: Inclusive and supportive organization with opportunities to immerse in research, professional development, and multicultural engagement. For all academic/career paths, SACNAS will connect you to the right people, programs, and resources for you to grow.

name: Cal Poly Heavy Civil Club
summary: 1. Community through education and professionalism. Leadership through ethics and knowledge. 2. The Cal Poly Heavy Civil Club (CPHCC) promotes and coordinates interaction between Heavy Civil Construction professionals and projects with students.

name: SLO Studios
summary: SLO Studios is aimed at producing short films, and content. Our vision is to provide a resource for students across all majors on campus to dip their feet into the vast world of filmmaking.

name: Cal Poly Archaeology Club
summary: The Cal Poly Archaeology Club aims to promote knowledge of the archaeological discipline and provide a network for members to connect with both professionals and their peers.

name: Engineers for Global Cultural Awareness
summary: Engineers for Global Awareness is a space for engineers to learn more about the different engineering practices worldwide.

name: Guardian Scholars
summary: Guardian Scholars, Office of Dean of Students

name: Cal Poly 3D Printed Aircraft Club
summary: Our club is dedicated to researching and constructing the next generation of 3D printed RC aircraft for both competition and materials research.

name: SGI-USA Buddhism
summary: We represent SGI-USA, a Buddhist community of practitioners of the humanistic philosophy of Nichiren Buddhism centered on respect for the dignity of life. From this foundation, we have empowering discussions with others about our hopes and challenges.

name: Sigma Pi Sigma at Cal Poly
summary: The Cal Poly SLO Chapter of The National Physics Honor Society Sigma Pi Sigma.

name: Gender Equity Center (GEC)
summary: Founded by students the Gender Equity Center empowers feminine, transgender, womxn-of-center, other gender-expansive students, and their supporters through expansive and inclusive programing. Register for this quarter’s events in our profile.

name: Pride Center (LGBTQIA2S)
summary: Empowering people of all sexual orientations, gender identities, and expressions; programming focuses on intersectional community, self-exploration, radical healing, and centering the QTPOC experience. Register for events in our profile.

name: Students with Dependents (DOS)
summary: A program of the Dean of Students to support Cal Poly Students with Dependents.

name: Educational Opportunity Program (EOP)
summary: Provides admission, academic and financial support services to historically underserved students throughout California.

name: Associated Students, Inc. (ASI)
summary: Connecting students to their ultimate college experience.

name: The Creative Anatomy Club
summary: The Creative Anatomy Club focuses on the intersection of anatomical science, human health and the arts, providing a space for individuals to celebrate their physical differences while engaging in creative and educational activities and events.

name: Regeneration Field Institute in San Luis Obispo
summary: RFI in SLO is an educational and immerse student organization dedicated to redefining the way we design structures and contribute to the environment. We study bamboo construction and opportunities abroad for hands-on experience working with the material.

name: PUENTE @ Cal Poly
summary: Puente @ Cal Poly is a community where Puente students and other Cal Poly students can feel connected through leadership, culture and community service opportunities, in order to cross the bridge to success!

name: Cal Poly Quantitative Finance Club
summary: An organization dedicated to educating students about the field of quantitative finance, including member led presentations, seminars lead by industry professionals, and hands on group projects.

name: The Lore Club
summary: Cal Poly Lore Club strives to create an inclusive environment to foster respectful discussions of various fictional works, ranging from videogames to literature. All opinions and theories are welcome, as multiple perspectives are key to understanding.

name: Mustang Consulting at Cal Poly
summary: The purpose of Mustang Consulting is to provide pro bono consulting services to various organizations, community groups, and other entities in need, while providing professional development opportunities for its members.

name: Men's Health & Wellbeing Club
summary: The Men’s Health & Wellbeing Club aims to educate, support, and unite young college men who are seeking to improve their lives in a holistic way. We provide resources, tools, and community to promote a healthy physical, mental, and social lifestyle.

name: Google Developer Student Clubs
summary: Google Developer Student Clubs (GDSC) are university-based community groups for students interested in Google technologies and programming. There are GDSCs in over 110 countries around the world that include undergraduate and graduate students.

name: Nuclear is Clean Energy
summary: We aim to educate club members, Cal Poly students, and members of the community about reactors, waste, and tech. Through events, public outreach, and talks we will engage with members of the public about the use of nuclear power in energy infrastructure.

name: Computing Club Collaborative
summary: Leaders from computing organizations on campus come together to create immersive events for Cal Poly students!

name: Ignite Consulting Junior Enterprise
summary: Junior Enterprises aims to provide students of higher education with a unique learn by doing experience through managing and working with consulting firms/businesses.

name: Women's Outdoor Adventure Club: Backcountry Squatters
summary: Backcountry Squatters is committed to fostering participation, leadership, and inclusivity among women and gender-queer folks within the outdoor industry and community.

name: Cal Poly Counter-Strike Club
summary: CPCS is Cal Poly's hub for all things Counter-Strike. We provide a community for all players, both casual and competitive! Join our discord for more info! https://discord.gg/PkPnZWmWAA

name: Delta Sigma Phi - Epsilon Rho Chapter
summary: Delta Sigma Phi's mission is "Building Better Men". The "Better Man" is defined as a man of courage, a man of action, and a man of excellence. Members of Delta Sigma Phi value culture, harmony, and friendship.

name: Asian Pacific Islander Desi American (APIDA) Initiatives
summary: Student Center and Community, part of Student Diversity and Belonging.

name: Future Opportunities through Research, Growth, and Entrepreneurship
summary: FORGE empowers students to innovate, lead, and solve real problems through STEM and entrepreneurship. We provide tools and a community for big ideas, fostering critical thinking, collaboration, and creativity.encouraging action, failure, and real impact.

name: Cal Poly Women in Electrical Engineering Club
summary: Cal Poly IEEE Women in Electrical Engineering Club is a support group for female Electrical Engineering majors at the California Polytechnic State University.

name: Alliance Foundation Student Club
summary: The club aims to promote interdisciplinary connections between students and industry professionals in the Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Planning fields.

name: NerdFlare
summary: NerdFlare is an interdisciplinary student group that teaches students how to design and create printed circuit board (PCB) artwork. We hold various beginner-friendly workshops covering programming, circuit design, and art.

name: CodeBox
summary: A club for builders and doers. CodeBox helps students bring project ideas to life by connecting them with teams, project leads, and tech leads. Whether it’s AI, web, or something totally new—if it’s fun and you’re passionate, this is your place.

name: South Asian Business Student Assosciation
summary: South Asian Business Student Association (SABSA) is a student-led org at Cal Poly SLO that empowers students through mentorship, cultural connection, and career development—all in an inclusive, no-pressure environment open to all majors and backgrounds.

name: BioBreakthrough
summary: We are a student-led organization dedicated to exploring nontraditional STEM career paths. Through our annual conference, we aim to connect students with professionals across diverse sectors and foster innovation and collaboration.

name: Sierra Madre & Yosemite Residence Halls
summary: Sierra Madre & Yosemite Residence Halls - Student Group: First year.

name: South Mountain Residence Halls
summary: South Mountain Residence Halls - Student Group: First years

name: Poly Canyon Village Apartments
summary: Poly Canyon Village Apartments - Student Groups: First year, Transfer, Graduate and Continuing students

name: Advanced Mathematics and Physics Society
summary: We are a collaborative student group aimed at studying advanced topics in mathematics and theoretical physics.

name: Computer Architecture Research Project at Cal Poly
summary: The Computer Architecture Research Project (CARP) at Cal Poly is a student-led community focused on education, mentorship, research, and open-source chip design. Our goal is to provide students with hands-on opportunities to explore computer architecture

name: Artificial Intelligence for Business
summary: The AI for Business Club exists to explore the applications of artificial intelligence across all areas of business. Through our meetings we aim to equip members with knowledge and experience in leveraging AI to drive innovation in business.

name: American Society for Quality
summary: The American Society for Quality (ASQ) is a global professional organization founded in 1946 that promotes quality management practices for individuals and organizations worldwide.

name: Cal Poly Birding Club
summary: Cal Poly Birding Club is a student community of new and seasoned birders alike! From campus to chaparral to the coast, we take field trips around SLO county to observe the birds we see along the way. Join the flock!

name: Aviation Machinery at Cal Poly
summary: A Cal Poly student organization dedicated to innovating, researching, and developing single-purpose drone technology through creative design, hands-on prototyping, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

name: Optica Student Chapter at Cal Poly
summary: The Optica Student Chapter at Cal Poly promotes optics and photonics through seminars, workshops, and outreach, connecting students with research, professional opportunities, and Optica’s global network.

name: Cal Poly Ethics and Sustainability in Business Club
summary: Ethics and Sustainability in Business Club promotes awareness, education, and meaningful engagement for college students in the fields of ethics and sustainability as they relate to business practices.
